_note: Slide-deck tour. intro = start lightbox. stages[] -> {name,badge,stops[]}. Each stop: title, image (ONE), caption?, lead, points[], layers[] (slugs shown), center [lng,lat] + zoom (frames the stop), or fit:'all'. cta?. 'Operating now' badge = green.

intro.title: Wells Gray — the Master Plan
intro.body:
393 acres of private land beside Wells Gray Provincial Park, near Clearwater, BC.

This guided tour walks through what's already built and operating, then the next phase of the plan — the maker space, cabins along the creek, the festival grounds, and an art-and-nature trail network.

A dozen short stops. Use the buttons to move; close anytime to explore the map yourself.

stops.title: Built & Operating
stops.lead: Five seasons in — the working prototype the village builds on.
points: Full-service & power/water RV sites, rental cabins & a glamping dome
A wood-fired sauna and a 120-person event gazebo with commercial kitchen
Private swimming lake, shower houses, hiking trails, horse corrals, golf & disc-golf
3,000+ visitors a year, with minimal marketing

stops.title: RV & Tiny-Home Sites
stops.lead: Full-service and power/water sites for RVs and tiny homes.
points: 45 serviced sites, plus power & water sites
Pull-through and back-in, shaded and open
The operating backbone of the campground

stops.title: The Sauna
stops.lead: A wood-fired cedar barrel sauna with a cold plunge.
points: Year-round — steam, then a cold dip by the creek
Anchors the wellness rhythm already running on site
A taste of the spa planned for a later phase

stops.title: Glamping Dome
stops.lead: A geodesic glamping dome for stays and gatherings.
points: Sleeps guests in comfort, open to the sky
Doubles as a space for movie nights and small events
One of the signature stays on the land

stops.title: The Bunk Cabins
stops.lead: Three rental bunk cabins tucked along the creek.
points: Simple, cozy stays — among the original built accommodation
Booked through the season alongside the dome
A short walk from the lake and gathering spaces

stops.title: The Event Gazebo
stops.lead: A 120-person event gazebo with a commercial kitchen.
points: Hosts weddings, gatherings and community meetups
Campfire and shared meals at the heart of it
The village's main indoor gathering space today

stops.title: Golf & Disc Golf
stops.lead: A 9-hole golf course that doubles as an 18-basket disc-golf course.
points: Golf or disc golf on the same scenic fairways
Mountain and forest views the whole way around
Open to guests and the public all season

stops.title: The Permaculture Garden
stops.lead: A working permaculture garden on the land.
points: Food, herbs and pollinators grown on site
A living classroom for regenerative growing
The seed of the larger regenerative farm in the vision

stops.title: Now — building the village
stops.lead: Everything so far is built and operating. From here on is the plan: Phase 2 turns the working campground into a village.
points: A maker space, creekside cabins, festival grounds and an art-trail network
Built in sequence — funded by the operating site and milled from its own cleared timber

stops.title: The Maker Space
stops.lead: A community workshop — and the engine of the build.
points: 3D printers, CNC, laser and woodworking
Fabricates and finishes the village's cabins and tiny homes

stops.title: Creekside Cabins
stops.lead: 38 small cabins set in rows along the creek.
points: Each plugs into existing power, water, septic & internet
Modern net-zero prefab units, finished in the maker space
A short walk from the campground core

stops.title: Lakeside Cabins
stops.lead: 31 cabins in two rows curving along the lake's edge.
points: 31 net-zero cabins in two rows, hugging the shoreline
Set back from the water, a short stroll to the lake
Finished in the maker space, plugged into existing services

stops.title: Event Gazebo
stops.lead: An octagonal timber pavilion for gatherings of every kind.
points: Hosts judo lessons, ceremonies, retreats and more
A flexible, open community space
Part of the Phase 2 community-buildings cluster

stops.title: The Event Stage
stops.lead: An outdoor stage for music, performance and festival nights.
points: A gathering point for shows, talks and celebrations
Scales up with the festival grounds
Part of the Phase 2 community buildings

stops.title: Festival Grounds
stops.lead: A leave-no-trace festival meadow — light-touch by design.
points: Clear the meadow and cut/clear the trails
Add a new shower house and grouped portapotties
Scales from 500 to 5,000 people, no permanent build
Cleared timber is milled on site for later-phase cabins

stops.title: Hiking & Art Trails
stops.lead: An art-and-nature hike threading the property together.
points: A cleared trail network through forest and along the creek
Art "landings" — installations and rest spots along the way
Links the campground, creekside cabins and festival meadow

stops.title: The Bigger Picture
stops.lead: Beyond these phases — a residential village, and a network.
points: A car-free wellness village, regenerative farm and lakeside spa are planned for later phases
Wells Gray is the flagship — a template for aligned villages elsewhere
